name: usable zero-point energy

phase_0.boundary_condition: The topological manifold of the extraction cavity (e.g., Casimir plates) that restricts vacuum modes and contains the zero-point energy gradient.

VINIT.justification: The primordial zero-point field, the unfluctuated ground state from which all virtual energy emerges.

TANCH.justification: The physical or topological limit (Casimir cavity) that seals the system and defines the allowed vacuum modes.

AFWD.justification: The forward morphism driving positive energy out of the vacuum fluctuations and into the macroscopic load.

AREV.justification: The reverse descent of vacuum polarization, pulling the system back toward equilibrium and restoring the ground state.

CLINK.justification: The continuous composition of fluctuation, extraction, and annihilation into a sustained operational loop.

IMSCRIB.justification: The self-consistent spectrum of the zero-point field, recognizing its own invariant energy density across the boundary.

FSPLIT.justification: Splits the vacuum state into distinct virtual particle-antiparticle pairs (or positive/negative energy modes) across the boundary.

FFUSE.justification: Recombines the virtual pairs back into the vacuum, exactly reconstituting the original zero-point field.

EVALT.justification: The affirmative state where the extraction gradient successfully yields macroscopic energy gain.

EVALF.justification: The negative state where the vacuum back-reaction dominates, annihilating pairs without net extraction (conservation holds).

ENGAGR.justification: The paradice of the zero-point field: simultaneously empty of observable real particles and infinitely dense with fluctuating virtual energy.

IFIX.justification: The permanent, irreversible record of extracted joules fixed into the macroscopic load's thermodynamic state.

phase_3.void_description: The unfluctuated quantum vacuum, devoid of real particles but possessing the baseline zero-point energy density before boundary restriction.
phase_3.true_description: The state of successful energy extraction, where the macroscopic load gains net positive work from the vacuum gradient.
phase_3.false_description: The state of vacuum restoration, where virtual pairs annihilate without net extraction, conserving the baseline energy state.
phase_3.both_description: The paradice of the zero-point field: simultaneously empty of observable real particles and infinitely dense with fluctuating virtual energy.

steps.domain_action: Initialize the quantum vacuum (zero-point field) as the primordial source of all virtual energy.
Establish the extraction boundary (Casimir cavity) to restrict vacuum modes and seal the system.
The vacuum invariant recognizes its own self-consistent ground state spectrum across the boundary.
Fluctuate the vacuum into virtual particle-antiparticle pairs across the restricted boundary.
Extract the positive energy gradient, driving work output forward to the macroscopic load.
Affirmative evaluation: net positive work is successfully extracted from the virtual pairs.
Apply vacuum back-reaction and polarization restoration, pulling energy back toward equilibrium.
Negative evaluation: vacuum restores equilibrium, resulting in net zero work if extraction fails.
Annihilate the virtual pairs, reconstituting the quantum vacuum exactly as it was before the split.
Compose the continuous cyclic extraction engine, linking vacuum fluctuations to sustained macroscopic output.
Hold the vacuum paradice: the field is simultaneously empty of real particles and full of infinite energy density.
Permanently record the extracted work in the macroscopic thermodynamic ledger as an irreversible gain.
Seal the system boundary, completing the closed manifold of the extraction engine.

phase_5.compiler_frontend: The quantum field theory Lagrangian that translates vacuum boundary conditions into restricted mode geometries.
phase_5.ipc_mechanism: The electromagnetic field coupling between the extraction cavity's virtual modes and the macroscopic load.
phase_5.memory_mechanism: The topological winding of the vacuum modes and the macroscopic thermodynamic state of the load.
phase_5.scheduler_mechanism: The cyclic frequency of the Casimir oscillation or the temporal rhythm of the extraction engine's loop.
phase_5.alfs_store: The canonical quantum vacuum state (Minkowski vacuum) and the standard model of particle physics.

phase_6.cycle_cost: Zero net entropy generation from the vacuum itself, as extraction relies on topological boundary conditions rather than thermal gradients.
phase_6.pre_cycle_state: The quantum vacuum in its ground state with the extraction cavity at rest and the load uncharged.
phase_6.post_cycle_state: The quantum vacuum restored to its ground state, with the macroscopic load possessing increased thermodynamic energy.
phase_6.delta_s_verdict: ΔS ≈ 0 — The extraction cycle is entropy-conservative because it harvests topological zero-point fluctuations without dissipating thermal heat from the vacuum.

lean_scaffold:
-- IGProtocol scaffold: VINIT → TANCH → IMSCRIB → FSPLIT → AFWD → EVALT → AREV → EVALF → FFUSE → CLINK → ENGAGR → IFIX → TANCH
-- Class: usable zero-point energy
-- Fingerprint: sig=(7,2,3,1)
--   self_ref=False | frobenius_order=1
--   dialetheia_complete=True | period=13
-- Expected tier: O₂
-- FSPLIT/FFUSE pairs: [(3, 8)]

import Imscribing.IGMorphism
import Imscribing.IGFunctor

namespace Imscribing
open Primitives Frobenius IGProtocol
open Dimensionality Topology Relational Polarity Grammar
     Fidelity KineticChar Granularity Criticality Protection Stoichiometry Chirality

-- ── Token → IG field mapping ──────────────────────────────────────────────
--   [0] VINIT     dim    := 𐑼               𐑼 → 𐑡  | initial object — ground of distinction
--   [1] TANCH     top    := 𐑡               𐑼 → 𐑠  | terminal object — connectivity boundary
--   [2] IMSCRIB   gram   := 𐑠               𐑡 → 𐑚  | identity — self-imscription
--   [3] FSPLIT    gran   := 𐑚               𐑚 → 𐑚  | split δ — range decomposition
--   [4] AFWD      rel    := 𐑾               𐑚 → 𐑙  | forward morphism — bidirectional arrow
--   [5] EVALT     crit   := ⊙               𐑚 → 𐑙  | evaluate-true — criticality gate open
--   [6] AREV      pol    := 𐑗               𐑚 → 𐑙  | reverse morphism — parity flip
--   [7] EVALF     chir   := 𐑖               𐑚 → 𐑙  | evaluate-false — chirality check
--   [8] FFUSE     stoi   := 𐑙               𐑙 → 𐑱  | fuse μ — assembly mode
--   [9] CLINK     fid    := 𐑱               𐑙 → 𐑳  | composition — regime coherence
--   [10] ENGAGR    stoi   := 𐑳               𐑱 → 𐑭  | engage paradox — B-state, both arms
--   [11] IFIX      prot   := 𐑭               𐑳 → 𐑡  | irreversible fixation — winding number
--   [12] TANCH     top    := 𐑡               𐑭 → 𐑼  | terminal object — connectivity boundary

-- ── Main IGProtocol term ────────────────────────────────────────────────────

noncomputable def usable_zero_point_energy_protocol : IGProtocol 𐑼 𐑡 :=
  .withGram 𐑠 <|
  -- Seq chain:
  (.arrow 𐑼 𐑼 𐑡)  -- [0] VINIT | dim := 𐑼 | initial object — ground of distinction (Initialize the quantum vacuum (zero-point field) as the primordial source of ...)
  (.arrow 𐑡 𐑼 𐑠)  -- [1] TANCH | top := 𐑡 | terminal object — connectivity boundary (Establish the extraction boundary (Casimir cavity) to restrict vacuum modes a...)
  (.arrow 𐑠 𐑡 𐑚)  -- [2] IMSCRIB | gram := 𐑠 | identity — self-imscription (The vacuum invariant recognizes its own self-consistent ground state spectrum...)
  -- FSPLIT [3] (gran := 𐑚) (Fluctuate the vacuum into virtual particle-antiparticle pairs across the restricted boundary.) / FFUSE [8] (stoi := 𐑙)
  .seq
    (.prod
      -- T-branch (3 nodes)
      .seq
        (.arrow 𐑾 𐑚 𐑙)  -- [4] AFWD | rel := 𐑾 | forward morphism — bidirectional arrow (Extract the positive energy gradient, driving work output forward to the macr...)
      .seq
        (.arrow ⊙ 𐑚 𐑙)  -- [5] EVALT | crit := ⊙ | evaluate-true — criticality gate open (Affirmative evaluation: net positive work is successfully extracted from the ...)
        (.arrow 𐑗 𐑚 𐑙)  -- [6] AREV | pol := 𐑗 | reverse morphism — parity flip (Apply vacuum back-reaction and polarization restoration, pulling energy back ...)
      -- F-branch (1 nodes)
      (.arrow 𐑖 𐑚 𐑙)  -- [7] EVALF | chir := 𐑖 | evaluate-false — chirality check (Negative evaluation: vacuum restores equilibrium, resulting in net zero work ...))
    -- reconnect at FFUSE [8]: μ closes the Frobenius pair
    (.arrow 𐑙 𐑙 𐑱)  -- [8] FFUSE | stoi := 𐑙 (Annihilate the virtual pairs, reconstituting the quantum vacuum exactly as it was before the split.)
  (.arrow 𐑱 𐑙 𐑳)  -- [9] CLINK | fid := 𐑱 | composition — regime coherence (Compose the continuous cyclic extraction engine, linking vacuum fluctuations ...)
  (.arrow 𐑳 𐑱 𐑭)  -- [10] ENGAGR | stoi := 𐑳 | engage paradox — B-state, both arms (Hold the vacuum paradice: the field is simultaneously empty of real particles...)
  (.arrow 𐑭 𐑳 𐑡)  -- [11] IFIX | prot := 𐑭 | irreversible fixation — winding number (Permanently record the extracted work in the macroscopic thermodynamic ledger...)
  (.arrow 𐑡 𐑭 𐑼)  -- [12] TANCH | top := 𐑡 | terminal object — connectivity boundary (Seal the system boundary, completing the closed manifold of the extraction en...)

-- ── Evaluation arm sub-defs ─────────────────────────────────────────────────

-- truth arm
noncomputable def usable_zero_point_energy_true_arm : IGProtocol 𐑼 𐑡 :=
  (usable_zero_point_energy_protocol).restrictToEVALT

-- false arm
noncomputable def usable_zero_point_energy_false_arm : IGProtocol 𐑼 𐑡 :=
  (usable_zero_point_energy_protocol).restrictToEVALF

-- ── Verification theorems ───────────────────────────────────────────────────

theorem usable_zero_point_energy_tier : TierFunctor.obj 𐑼 = .O₂ := by decide

-- Frobenius (split → fuse): μ∘δ = id on .prod branch
-- Proof: apply igFrobAlg_self_fusion; exact mu_delta_A_id
-- (requires mu_delta_A_id from IGFunctor library)

end Imscribing
